zh.lifeSummary: 當Lord John Le Neveu Darcy I於1283年6月15日出生在英國林可爾史爾時，他的父親Sir Roger de Darcy24歲，母親Isabel De Aton Lady d'Arcy12歲。他於1315年之前與Emmeline Heron在英國結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1335年住在英國諾福爾可。他於1336年登記服兵役。他於1323年在愛爾蘭被列為justiciar 。他去世於1347年5月30日在英國越可爾吉斯波洛享年63歲，埋葬在英國越可爾吉斯波洛。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lord John Le Neveu Darcy I于1283年6月15日出生在英国林可爾史爾时，他的父亲Sir Roger de Darcy24岁，母亲Isabel De Aton Lady d'Arcy12岁。他于1315年之前与Emmeline Heron在英国结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1335年住在英国諾福爾可。他于1336年登记服兵役。他于1323年在爱尔兰被列为justiciar 。他去世于1347年5月30日在英国越可爾吉斯波洛享年63岁，埋葬在英国越可爾吉斯波洛。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Neveu
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Lucien, Normand, Aliette, Andre, Marcel.
French: relationship name from Old French neveu ‘nephew’, also ‘grandson’, used to distinguish the two bearers of the same personal name. Compare Neveau and Neveux .

variants.name: Neveux
meanings: French: variant of   Neveu   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Neveau
meanings: Altered form of French   Neveu   ‘nephew’, also ‘grandson’.

nameMeanings.name: Darcy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Eamon, Brendan, Eamonn, Maeve, Malachy, Niamh, Seamus.  French Gisele, Julien, Normand, Alain, Armand, Cecile, Clemence, Dominique, Emile, Gilles, Jean-Paul, Laure.
English (Lancashire) and Irish: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Arcy in Manche, France, with fused preposition d(e) ‘from’.

Irish: in Ireland, this is generally the Norman name (see 1 above), established in Meath by Sir William D'Arcy in the 1360s, but it was also adopted for Ó Dorchaidhe ‘descendant of the dark one’, from dorcha ‘dark, gloomy’, a Connacht name that fell together with the Norman surname, which was introduced by Sir William D'Arcy and Sir John D'Arcy, Chief Justiciar of Ireland in the 14th century.

variants.name: Darsey
meanings: Irish: variant of   Darcy   2.
Scottish: from Dairsie in Fife.

variants.name: Darcey
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and Irish (Tipperary): variant of   Darcy  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Lord John Le Neveu Darcy I was born on 15 June 1283, in Lincolnshire, England, his father, Sir Roger de Darcy, was 24 and his mother, Isabel De Aton Lady d'Arcy, was 12. He married Emmeline Heron before 1315, in England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He lived in Norfolk, England in 1335. He registered for military service in 1336. In 1323, his occupation is listed as justiciar  in Ireland. He died on 30 May 1347, in Guisborough, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 63, and was buried in Guisborough, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lord John Le Neveu Darcy I est né le 15 juin 1283, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Roger de Darcy, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Isabel De Aton Lady d'Arcy, avait 12 ans. Il a épousé Emmeline Heron avant 1315, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il a vécu à Norfolk, Angleterre en 1335. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1336. En 1323, sa profession de son indiquée est justiciar  à Irlande. Il est décédé le 30 mai 1347, à Guisborough, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Guisborough, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lord John Le Neveu Darcy I am 15. Juni 1283 in Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Roger de Darcy 24 und seine Mutter Isabel De Aton Lady d'Arcy 12. Er hat vor 1315 in England Emmeline Heron geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. 1335 hat er in Norfolk, England gelebt. Er hat sich 1336 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1323 ist sein Beruf als justiciar  in Irland aufgeführt. Er starb am 30. Mai 1347 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Guisborough, Yorkshire, England und wurde in Guisborough, Yorkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lord John Le Neveu Darcy I è nato il il 15 giugno 1283 nel Lincolnshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Roger de Darcy, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Isabel De Aton Lady d'Arcy, aveva 12 anni. Lui sposato Emmeline Heron prima del 1315, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Vissuto a Norfolk, England nel 1335. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1336. Nel 1323, la sua occupazione è indicata come justiciar  nel Irlanda. Morì il 30 maggio 1347, nel Guisborough, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 63 anni, e fu sepolto a Guisborough, Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Lord John Le Neveu Darcy Iが1283年6月15日にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir Roger de Darcyは24歳，母Isabel De Aton Lady d'Arcyは12歳であった。1315年以前にイギリスでEmmeline Heronと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1335年にはイギリスノーフォルクに住んでいた。彼は1336年に兵役登録をした。1323年，アイルランドでの職業はjusticiar と記録されている。彼は1347年5月30日にイギリスヨークシュアイアグイスボロウで63歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアグイスボロウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lord John Le Neveu Darcy I(이)가 1283년 6월 15일 영국 링코실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Roger de Darcy(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel De Aton Lady d'Arcy(은)는 12세였다. 그는 1315년 이전 영국에서 Emmeline Heron(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1335년도에 영국 노르폴크에서 살았다. 그는 1336년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1323년, 그의 직업은 아일랜드에서 justiciar 이었다. 그는 1347년 5월 30일 영국 요르크실르 길스버루그h에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 길스버루그h에 묻혔다.

pt.15 June 1283–30 May 1347: 15 de junho de 1283–30 de maio de 1347
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lord John Le Neveu Darcy I nasceu em 15 de junho de 1283, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Roger de Darcy, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Isabel De Aton Lady d'Arcy, tinha 12 anos. Ele casou-se com Emmeline Heron antes 1315, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ele viveu em Norfolk, Inglaterra em 1335. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1336. Em 1323, sua profissão aparece como justiciar  em Irlanda. Ele faleceu em 30 de maio de 1347, em Guisborough, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 63 anos, e foi sepultado em Guisborough, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lord John Le Neveu Darcy I родился 15 июня 1283 г. в местности линколншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Roger de Darcy было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Isabel De Aton Lady d'Arcy, 12. Он женился на женщине по имени Emmeline Heron перед 1315 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он жил в местности норфолк, Англия в 1335 году. В 1336 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1323 годуработал по профессии justiciar  в местности Ирландия. Он умер 30 мая 1347 г. в местности гуисбороую, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности гуисбороую, еркшир, Англия.

es.15 June 1283–30 May 1347: 15 de junio de 1283–30 de mayo de 1347
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lord John Le Neveu Darcy I nació el 15 de junio de 1283, en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Roger de Darcy, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Isabel De Aton Lady d'Arcy, tenía 12 años. Se casó con Emmeline Heron antes de 1315, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Vivió en Norfolk, Inglaterra en 1335. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1336. En 1323, su ocupación registrada era justiciar  en Irlanda. Murió el 30 de mayo de 1347, en Guisborough, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultado en Guisborough, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
